text.title: Domain-Driven Design (DDD) Worked Example
text.summary: One end-to-end worked example showing how arch-pattern-ddd produces a valid spec artefact.

example.title: Happy-path application
example.code:
--- artefact_id: ddd-context-map-shop-2026-05-23 owner: Ruslan Faion <ruslan@faion.net> ---
# Shop — DDD Context Map
## Bounded Contexts - Catalog - Ordering - Fulfilment - Billing
## Aggregates (Ordering) - `Order` (root) - invariants: - `total == sum(line_items.subtotal) + tax - discounts` - state == SUBMITTED -> no line item add/remove - entities: `LineItem` - value objects: `Money`, `ShippingAddress`
## Domain Events - `OrderSubmitted(order_id, customer_id, total)` - `OrderShipped(order_id, tracking_number)` - `OrderRefunded(order_id, amount)`
## Context Relationships - Catalog → Ordering: Open Host (REST GET /items/{id}) - Ordering → Fulfilment: Domain Event (`OrderSubmitted`) - Ordering → Billing: Domain Event (`OrderSubmitted`)
## Glossary | Term | Definition | Context | |------|------------|---------| | Order | Submitted basket awaiting fulfilment | Ordering | | Item | SKU + price + availability | Catalog | | LineItem | Product + qty in an Order | Ordering | | Shipment | Pick-pack-ship lifecycle of an Order | Fulfilment | | Invoice | Billing document for an Order | Billing |
example.note: Demonstrates the rules from 01-core-rules.xml applied to a concrete input; output validates against the schema in 02-output-contract.xml.
